hints.text: All coverage = **Gen3, white car w/o antenna** (back visible almost everywhere)

shared_with.note: Same car; Eswatini is also Gen3-only.
shared_with.diff: Tall grass → Eswatini; treeless tabletops → Lesotho.
Flat dry Kalahari → Botswana; mountains → Lesotho.

shared_with.note: Same car, but ZA also has Gen2 + Gen4 (Gen3 is rare there).
shared_with.diff: Gen3 southern-Africa round → lean the small three.

hints.text: **'Lesotho mountains'**: bare, treeless, tabletop-like shapes w/ **horizontal rocky ridgelines** (inland SE: more rolling green hills but ridges still show)
hints.uniq_note: Western-Cape ZA mountains can look similar — the everywhere-ness of the tabletops is the Lesotho tell.

hints.text: Plates **long white w/ blue text**; partially unblurred you may see the **Mokorotlo hat symbol** left
**Yellow outer + white inner lines**; some roads have the southern-African **triple middle lines** (2 solid + dashed between); drives **left**
Rural areas: traditional **round huts w/ thatched roofs** (rarer in the neighbours)
**Agave plants** common countryside clue; grasslands = **short grass**
Many people wear the traditional **Basotho woollen blanket**; lots of free-roaming **livestock** on roads (cows, horses, donkeys)
hints.uniq_note: The Basotho blanket is a Lesotho-only garment.

hints.text: Coverage very sparse (SE mostly yellow roads only; **all urban coverage in the NW**); two landscape halves: **'flat'** (tabletops far from the road; most coverage/farms) vs **mountainous** (road on the mountainside, big green mountains — only ~6 main roads covered, learnable by heart)
**Reservoirs**: the big **Katse** reservoir visible from the A25; smaller **Mohale** from an A3 off-road (don't confuse w/ the Letseng diamond-mine lake)

**Road spotlights**: A2 Maseru↔Mafeteng = by far the best pavement in the country; A25 east of Katse = **raindrops on the camera** in one direction; A5 south of Nyakosoba = construction w/ white-painted stick 'bollards' + wide dark-brown dirt road; Ramatsediso→Sehlabathebe NP = unpaved light-brown road, fluffy clouds, grassy high mountains + light exposed rocks
**Maseru** = the only real urban downtown (suburbs still rural); **Qacha's Nek** (remote SE border town) = green mountains all around + notably bad weather
